title: Messages not shown on opening of a chat
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

When I open a chat, The name of the contact and the input zone for a new message are often only shown. None existing messages are show. 
I must scroll down with a finger to show all the messages of this chat.
I do not know if this issue have the same cause of the #14524 bug that I have also.

This issue occurred randomly.

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Device

Huawei PRA-LX1

### Android version

8.0

### Signal version

8.3.4

### Link to debug log

_No response_

title: Deadlock on startup in v8.4.1: ReentrantSessionLock + JobManager.waitUntilInitialized + SQLiteConnectionPool
body:
## Bug Description

Signal v8.4.1 deadlocks on every cold start, making the app completely unusable. The deadlock occurs within ~5 seconds of launch and is 100% reproducible across force-stops and reboots. The issue began after updating to v8.4.1 (from a long-running install, first installed Oct 2022). Backups were recently enabled, which may be a contributing factor.

## Deadlock Analysis

Signal's own `DeadlockDetector` fires immediately on startup. The root cause is a circular dependency between three locks:

### 1. `JobManager.waitUntilInitialized` (JobManager.java:500)
Multiple startup tasks block here because the JobManager needs the database to initialize, but the database is locked.

### 2. `SQLiteDatabase.beginTransaction` (SQLiteDatabase.java:294)
The SQLite connection pool is fully starved. Dozens of threads wait 300-570+ seconds for a connection that never becomes available. Thread names include: `signal-bounded-0` through `3`, `signal-unbounded-2` through `19`, `signal-io-bounded-0`, `archive-db`, `pool-28-thread-1`.

### 3. `ReentrantSessionLock.acquire` (ReentrantSessionLock.java:18)
Multiple job runners are stuck waiting for the crypto session lock.

### Circular Dependency

The deadlock cycle is:
- **JobManager** can't initialize â†’ needs DB
- **DB** is locked by transactions that need the **session lock**
- **Session lock** is held by a job that needs the **JobManager** to be initialized

### Threads Involved

**Stuck on ReentrantSessionLock:**
- `SendDeliveryReceiptJob.onRun` (SendDeliveryReceiptJob.java:128)
- `ReactionSendJob` â†’ `GroupSendUtil.sendResendableDataMessage` (GroupSendUtil.java:631)
- `MultiDeviceConfigurationUpdateJob.onRun` (MultiDeviceConfigurationUpdateJob.java:107)
- `MultiDeviceBlockedUpdateJob.onRun` (MultiDeviceBlockedUpdateJob.kt:59)
- `IncomingMessageObserver.MessageRetrievalThread` (IncomingMessageObserver.kt:456)

**Stuck on SQLiteDatabase.beginTransaction:**
- `BackupRepository.maybeFixAnyDanglingUploadProgress` (BackupRepository.kt:619)
- `MessageSendLogTables.trimOldMessages` (MessageSendLogTables.kt:385)
- `NotificationChannels.ensureCustomChannelConsistency` (NotificationChannels.java:572)
- `InAppPaymentTable.consumeBackupPaymentsToNotifyUser` (InAppPaymentTable.kt:24)
- `InAppPaymentTable.consumeDonationPaymentsToNotifyUser` (InAppPaymentTable.kt:24)
- `ConversationListDataSource.load` (ConversationListDataSource.java:107)
- `RemappedRecordTables.getAllRecipientMappings` (RemappedRecordTables.kt:24)

**Stuck on JobManager.waitUntilInitialized:**
- `ApplicationContext.beginJobLoop` (ApplicationContext.java:517)
- `RemoteConfig.refreshIfNecessary` (RemoteConfig.kt:116)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Long-running Signal install (since Oct 2022) with large message history
2. Enable backups
3. Update to v8.4.1
4. App deadlocks on every cold start

## What Was Tried

- Force-stop and relaunch: deadlock recurs within 5 seconds every time
- Clear cache: no effect
- Launch with WiFi/network disabled: no effect (backup code still tries to check DB)
- The app also crashes (twice observed) when attempting to submit debug logs, since `FastJobStorage.debugAdditionalDetails` also needs the locked DB

## Environment

- Signal version: 8.4.1 (updated 2026-03-21)
- Device: Pixel (arm64), Android, density 420dpi
- First install: 2022-10-17

## Logcat Evidence

### DeadlockDetector trace (within 5 seconds of cold start):
```
W DeadlockDetector: org.thoughtcrime.securesms.backup.v2.BackupRepository.maybeFixAnyDanglingUploadProgress(BackupRepository.kt:619)
W DeadlockDetector: org.thoughtcrime.securesms.jobmanager.JobManager.waitUntilInitialized(JobManager.java:500)
W DeadlockDetector: org.thoughtcrime.securesms.database.MessageSendLogTables.trimOldMessages(MessageSendLogTables.kt:385)
W DeadlockDetector: org.thoughtcrime.securesms.ApplicationContext.beginJobLoop(ApplicationContext.java:517)
```

### SQLiteConnectionPool starvation (monotonically increasing):
```
W SQLiteConnectionPool: ...signal.db has been unable to grant a connection to thread 2464 (archive-db) with flags 0x2 for 570.021 seconds.
W SQLiteConnectionPool: ...signal.db has been unable to grant a connection to thread 107 (signal-bounded-1) with flags 0x2 for 540.024 seconds.
```
clean_body:

## Deadlock Analysis

Signal's own `DeadlockDetector` fires immediately on startup. The root cause is a circular dependency between three locks:

### 1. `JobManager.waitUntilInitialized` (JobManager.java:500)
Multiple startup tasks block here because the JobManager needs the database to initialize, but the database is locked.

### 2. `SQLiteDatabase.beginTransaction` (SQLiteDatabase.java:294)
The SQLite connection pool is fully starved. Dozens of threads wait 300-570+ seconds for a connection that never becomes available. Thread names include: `signal-bounded-0` through `3`, `signal-unbounded-2` through `19`, `signal-io-bounded-0`, `archive-db`, `pool-28-thread-1`.

### 3. `ReentrantSessionLock.acquire` (ReentrantSessionLock.java:18)

The deadlock cycle is:
- **JobManager** can't initialize â†’ needs DB
- **DB** is locked by transactions that need the **session lock**
- **Session lock** is held by a job that needs the **JobManager** to be initialized

### Threads Involved

**Stuck on ReentrantSessionLock:**
- `SendDeliveryReceiptJob.onRun` (SendDeliveryReceiptJob.java:128)
- `ReactionSendJob` â†’ `GroupSendUtil.sendResendableDataMessage` (GroupSendUtil.java:631)

- Clear cache: no effect

## Environment

- Device: Pixel (arm64), Android, density 420dpi
- First install: 2022-10-17

## Logcat Evidence

### DeadlockDetector trace (within 5 seconds of cold start):
```
W DeadlockDetector: org.thoughtcrime.securesms.backup.v2.BackupRepository.maybeFixAnyDanglingUploadProgress(BackupRepository.kt:619)
W DeadlockDetector: org.thoughtcrime.securesms.jobmanager.JobManager.waitUntilInitialized(JobManager.java:500)

W DeadlockDetector: org.thoughtcrime.securesms.ApplicationContext.beginJobLoop(ApplicationContext.java:517)
```

### SQLiteConnectionPool starvation (monotonically increasing):
```
W SQLiteConnectionPool: ...signal.db has been unable to grant a connection to thread 2464 (archive-db) with flags 0x2 for 570.021 seconds.

```

title: Android tablet cannot be linked as a secondary device on Android 16 / One UI 8
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

Steps to reproduce:
1. Install Signal on Galaxy Tab S8 (SM-X700)
2. Launch app (fresh install, no backup restore)
3. Observe initial screen and phone number entry screen

Expected:
Option to "Link to existing account" or QR code for linking

Actual:
Only phone number registration is available
Overflow menu only shows "Use proxy"
No QR code or linking option anywhere

Symptoms when trying to link the tablet
- No â€œLink deviceâ€ or QR option anywhere
- Only phone registration path
- Linked Devices menu unavailable (when unregistered)

What I've tried
- Reinstall / clear data
- Airplane mode
- Deny all permissions
- No SIM
- Disabled backup
- Checked overflow menu (it only shows "use proxy"

Device + OS
- Galaxy Tab S8 (SM-X700)
- Android 16
- One UI 8
- Feb 2026 security patch
- Signal version 8.3.4

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Device

Galaxy Tab S8 (SM-X700)

### Android version

16

### Signal version

8.3.4

### Link to debug log

_No response_

title: Messages received on desktop never show up on phone (new contacts only?)
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

I've had this happen multiple times over the past few months. I use the desktop client on OSX and the Android app on my phone.

1. A new contact sends me messages
2. The messages are received on my desktop (not in use at that time, but the computer is on)
3. My phone never receives these messages
4. If I respond to the messages, only the new messages from that point onwards show up

Looking at the time this happened (2026-03-19 14:31:34), I see a bunch of "Decryption error" showing up. In this case it was 3 messages that I missed and these errors seem to happen after 3 envelopes are received so that does add up.

### Link to debug log

https://debuglogs.org/android/8.2.2/e91121f66743871950286dbef0e9f78abe4cbf5732392ab5324ba025fd98f129

title: Sending photo from in-app camera causes deadlock
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

Hit a bug where when I try to send a picture taken with the in-app camera (camera icon from chat list), select a contact and hit send, a spinning icon appears over the image that seem to spin forever (no image sent). Seems to be hitting a deadlock.

When backing out/relaunching the app, I am prevented from e.g. sending messages (and I assume other things aren't working correctly at that point as well). The UI still allows for typing messages and hitting send, the messages disappear from the input field when hitting send, but no messages appear in the chat. Desktop continued to work throughout this.

This is around when the problem occurred:
```
[8.2.2] [26   ] 2026-03-19 17:40:48.045 GMT+01:00 W DeadlockDetector: Found a long block! Blocked for at least 5000 ms.
```

After rebooting the phone, everything seems to be working again and that is when I collected the debug log. Force stopping the app and reopening it also seems to fix the problem, but not simply "swiping it away". Using the camera button inside the input field of a specific chat works as intended. I am able to reproduce the bug through the following steps:

1. Open Signal
2. Choose camera icon
3. Take picture
4. Hit send
5. Choose contact
6. Hit send

Not a critical issue as it is possible to work around (and avoid).

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Device

Pixel 9 Pro

### Android version

16 (GrapheneOS)

### Signal version

8.2.2

### Link to debug log

https://debuglogs.org/android/8.2.2/d21c3f425a35c64565cd8afd60ab57e5f44d14019f944e944674a3a37a53e286

title: A limit of 3 pinned messages causes silent data loss and poor UX
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

I recently started using the message pinning feature and ran into a serious usability issue that led to unintended loss of important information.

There appears to be a hard limit of **3 pinned messages per chat**, but this limitation is not clearly communicated within the app (Android or Desktop). While this information exists on the website, there is **no in-app warning or indication** when the limit is reached.

**What happened:**

* I pinned multiple messages over time, assuming (based on experience with other apps) that there was no strict limit.
* After pinning more than 3 messages, older pinned messages were silently removed.
* There was **no warning, confirmation dialog, or explanation** that pinning a new message would unpin or overwrite an older one.
* As a result, previously pinned messages were unpinned without my knowledge. These were messages I had pinned specifically because they were important, and now I donâ€™t know what they were or how to find them again.

**Why this is a problem:**

* This behavior leads to **unexpected data loss** from a user perspective.
* The lack of feedback or warning creates a **poor and confusing user experience**.
* A limit of 3 pinned messages is **very restrictive**, especially for users who rely on pinning to organize important content.

**Suggestions for improvement:**

1. **Add an in-app warning or confirmation dialog** when attempting to pin a message beyond the limit (e.g., "You can only pin 3 messages. Pinning this will remove the oldest pinned message.").
2. **Display the current pin limit clearly in the UI**, so users are aware of the constraint.
3. Consider **increasing the maximum number of pinned messages**, or making it configurable if possible.
4. Optionally, provide a **history or recovery option** for recently unpinned messages to prevent accidental loss.

**Expected behavior:**

* Users should be clearly informed about limitations before actions result in loss of pinned items.
* The app should prevent silent overwriting of previously pinned messages without user awareness.

This issue directly undermines the purpose of the pinning feature, which is to help users quickly access important messages

Thank you for considering improvements to this functionality.

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Device

_No response_

### Android version

_No response_

### Signal version

_No response_

### Link to debug log

_No response_

title: App pesters user to enable Signal Secure Backups
body: ### Guidelines - [x] I have searched searched open and closed issues for duplicates - [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended - [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic ### Bug description The app currently periodically presents the user with two severely misleading in-app notifications encouraging them to enable Signal Secure Backups. While I strongly believe that these should be one-time notifications, there is an even stronger case that they should be disabled entirely if the user has already enabled On-device Backups. Currently neither is the case. Signal Secure Backups, while a nice feature for the average user, are not compatible with every threat model. Repeated pestering to enable the feature is a user-hostile UX decision. In addition to being severely misleading, these notifications cannot be permanently dismissed or disabled. While I myself care enough to manually tap "Not now" at least once per week, this will obviously lead to most users eventually giving in and enabling this compromising feature. ### Screenshots ### Device Pixel 10 Pro XL ### Android version 16 ### Signal version 8.2.2 ### Link to debug log _No response_
clean_body: The app currently periodically presents the user with two severely misleading in-app notifications encouraging them to enable Signal Secure Backups. While I strongly believe that these should be one-time notifications, there is an even stronger case that they should be disabled entirely if the user has already enabled On-device Backups. Currently neither is the case. Signal Secure Backups, while a nice feature for the average user, are not compatible with every threat model. Repeated pestering to enable the feature is a user-hostile UX decision. In addition to being severely misleading, these notifications cannot be permanently dismissed or disabled. While I myself care enough to manually tap "Not now" at least once per week, this will obviously lead to most users eventually giving in and enabling this compromising feature. ### Screenshots

title: Pin restore brings back an contact name into a state that the user can't edit
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

## Description:
Users are in a state where contact names were once imported from system contacts, but can no longer be edited or effaced, nor are they able to see the contact's profile name as they are known to others in common groups.

## Repro Steps
Setup:
Local party:
Android 16 on Pixel 6a
Signal 8.2.1 available from the Play Store but not yet installed
Has phone number PN_L

Remote party is on phone number PN_A, already registered with Signal with "allow people to find me by phone number" enabled.

### Phase 1: Get the name from contacts into Signal
1. On the local party, create a local contact card for PN_A, name it "Government Name".
2. Download, install Signal, and do an initial registration with PN_L, allowing access to contacts, and creating a pin. Backup is not setup.
3. In signal, compose a message. See that PN_A is already registered with Signal and can be messaged by "Government Name" obtained from local contacts.
4. Local sends a message to "Government Name". (PN_A), creating a PNI to PNI session. 

### Phase 2, restore the contacts name from Signal Secure Value Recovery
5. Local resets their state: Deletes the signal app. Renames the PN_A contact card to "Dawn"
6.  Local redownloads Signal. On initial setup:
    6a Disallows contacts access. 
    6b Registers PN_L with Signal
    6c Declines to restore from backup (was not setup)
    6d restores with the pin setup in step 2.
7. The remote party replies on the PNI to PNI session created in step 4. Clicks through the safety number changed warning.
8. Local observes a message coming in from "Government Name".

## Expected behavior
Step 8 is possibly the expected behavior. This is the name previously known to the user, it was preserved and restored with the pin restore. However....

1. Users can no longer delete "Government Name" from their signal app, even if they changed it in their contacts. Enabling contacts access from Android settings does not overwrite "Government Name" in Signal with the new "Dawn" from local contacts. (It's not obvious to me that the android app is aware of a contacts permissions change made from android settings, or of a way to tell it to start ingesting contacts data to overwrite this bad state.) Nonetheless the preferable user control is in the Signal app, not in an upstream data source in Contacts.
    1a. Users may hide "Government Name" by setting a nickname, but falls back to "Government Name" if the nickname is deleted, indicating that data is still present
2. Users have no way of indicating their preference of seeing PN_A's profile name, putting them in an inconsistent state with other members of shared groups. This puts the local user at grave risk of outing "Government Name" to other members of the group as they aren't aware of the profile name that PN_A is presenting as to the group.

## Desired behavior
1. Users have a way to efface imported contact names
2. Users can choose to see others' profile name. 
3. Users are prevented from accidentally disclosing private names through ux that establishes the profile name as a the safe shared terminology for a given user in a group.

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Device

Pixel 6a

### Android version

16

### Signal version

8.2.1

### Link to debug log

_No response_
clean_body:
## Description:
Users are in a state where contact names were once imported from system contacts, but can no longer be edited or effaced, nor are they able to see the contact's profile name as they are known to others in common groups.

## Repro Steps
Setup:
Local party:
Android 16 on Pixel 6a
Signal 8.2.1 available from the Play Store but not yet installed
Has phone number PN_L

Remote party is on phone number PN_A, already registered with Signal with "allow people to find me by phone number" enabled.

### Phase 1: Get the name from contacts into Signal
1. On the local party, create a local contact card for PN_A, name it "Government Name".
2. Download, install Signal, and do an initial registration with PN_L, allowing access to contacts, and creating a pin. Backup is not setup.
3. In signal, compose a message. See that PN_A is already registered with Signal and can be messaged by "Government Name" obtained from local contacts.
4. Local sends a message to "Government Name". (PN_A), creating a PNI to PNI session. 

### Phase 2, restore the contacts name from Signal Secure Value Recovery
5. Local resets their state: Deletes the signal app. Renames the PN_A contact card to "Dawn"
6.  Local redownloads Signal. On initial setup:
    6a Disallows contacts access. 
    6b Registers PN_L with Signal
    6c Declines to restore from backup (was not setup)
    6d restores with the pin setup in step 2.
7. The remote party replies on the PNI to PNI session created in step 4. Clicks through the safety number changed warning.
8. Local observes a message coming in from "Government Name".

## Expected behavior
Step 8 is possibly the expected behavior. This is the name previously known to the user, it was preserved and restored with the pin restore. However....

1. Users can no longer delete "Government Name" from their signal app, even if they changed it in their contacts. Enabling contacts access from Android settings does not overwrite "Government Name" in Signal with the new "Dawn" from local contacts. (It's not obvious to me that the android app is aware of a contacts permissions change made from android settings, or of a way to tell it to start ingesting contacts data to overwrite this bad state.) Nonetheless the preferable user control is in the Signal app, not in an upstream data source in Contacts.
    1a. Users may hide "Government Name" by setting a nickname, but falls back to "Government Name" if the nickname is deleted, indicating that data is still present
2. Users have no way of indicating their preference of seeing PN_A's profile name, putting them in an inconsistent state with other members of shared groups. This puts the local user at grave risk of outing "Government Name" to other members of the group as they aren't aware of the profile name that PN_A is presenting as to the group.

## Desired behavior
1. Users have a way to efface imported contact names
2. Users can choose to see others' profile name. 
3. Users are prevented from accidentally disclosing private names through ux that establishes the profile name as a the safe shared terminology for a given user in a group.

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Device

Pixel 6a

### Android version

16

### Signal version

8.2.1

_No response_

title: Microphone and camera icon disappear after sending voice message
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

After sending a voice message, both the microphone and camera icons disappear.  I am unable to send another voice message.  To make them reappear, I have to exit and reenter the chat, or type and erase a message.  I've tried clearing the app cache and uninstalling/reinstalling.  This does not fix the problem.  This happens in all my chats with all my contacts.

title: Signal doesn't detect Google Play Services
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

I know that this has been reported before, but this issue happens on the stock pixel 8 pro os and on my OnePlus Ace 6T (converted to global). This wouldn't be such an issue if it weren't draining my battery in the background (even tho it's not that much). 
Edit: 
After reading the logs, it seems to be detecting play services, but couldn't find where the SERVICE_INVALID error code 9 could be. Also, some play feature like automatically entering the sms code is working (idk if that means anything)
FYI Uninstalled and reinstalled often signal both on Pixel and OnePlus

title: "Learn more" on the Signal Secure Backups set up screen links to wrong page
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

Go to Settings > Backups > Set up Signal Secure Backups.

At the bottom of the screen below "Enable backups" there is a "Learn more" button. This links to the support page [Backup and Restore Messages](https://support.signal.org/hc/en-us/articles/360007059752-Backup-and-Restore-Messages). This page does not talk about Signal Secure Backups. I'd expect the button to link to this [Signal Secures Backups](https://support.signal.org/hc/en-us/articles/9708267671322-Signal-Secure-Backups) support page.

### Screenshots

### Device

### Android version

8.0.4

title: Message context menu appears but options can't be selected
body:
### Guidelines

- [x] I have searched searched open and closed issues for duplicates
- [x] I am submitting a bug report for existing functionality that does not work as intended
- [x] This isn't a feature request or a discussion topic

### Bug description

When long-pressing on an individual message, the emoji menu and the message context menu (with options "reply", "forward", "copy" etc) both appear as expected. The emoji menu works correctly (emojis can be selected) but the message context menu does not work. No option can be selected. This is clearly unintended behavior.

I tried resetting Android zoom / text zoom, but doesn't change the issue.

### Screenshots

- [x] I have searched searched open and closed issues for duplicates

### Bug description

I restored my Signal account on a new phone from the local backup and shortly after I got error message that "Something went wrong with your username". I'm not sure if it's really related to account restoring, but it was the firs time I got such error since usernames was beta feature.
